rubric.evidence: Real headless full-lifecycle run (persistent profile, WASM q8): first visit showed loader state=download-required with a 'Download model (~850 MB)' button; after downloading, the model auto-transitioned to ready and a real translation ran — 'The weather is nice today.' (en_XX → fr_XX) → 'Le temps est agréable aujourd'hui.' 0 console errors. Runnability pre-verified separately with the same pipeline('translation','Xenova/mbart-large-50-many-to-many-mmt',{device:'wasm',dtype:'q8'}) returning a real translation.
rubric.notes: mBART-50 many-to-many; distinct from the built M2M100/Marian/NLLB translators by its denoising-pretraining lineage (BART scaled multilingual).

rubric.evidence: conformance io-shape-semantic=manual. A real run produces text in the target language that preserves the source meaning: EN→FR 'Le temps est agréable aujourd'hui.' is a correct, fluent French rendering. Token counts and the src→tgt codes are shown in the readout + see-inside panel.
rubric.notes: Many-to-many verified conceptually: any pair goes directly (no English pivot) via src_lang/tgt_lang.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/mbart-large-50-many-to-many-mmt, linked to card), declares-task (translation), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker: mbart.js MBartEngine → worker.js with a TextStreamer) all pass. At-a-glance documents the ~610M BART-style encoder-decoder and the 50-language code scheme.
rubric.notes: Size stated honestly as ~850 MB with a 'large model / may be slow or fail on a constrained phone' note.

rubric.evidence: conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED: after the first-visit download, a same-profile reload auto-initialised to state=ready with NO Download button (cachedHasDownloadBtn=false) and 0 console errors; the dark-media cached page also auto-inited ready. First visit correctly showed download-required + a Download button.
rubric.notes: Uses shared createModelLoader; no hand-rolled Load button. Per-model 'Clear cached model' control present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback all pass + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader progress bar; run status uses role=status aria-live=polite; translation failures + invalid/identical language pairs surface as an .err status; createModelLoader Retry covers load recovery. WASM model needs no WebGPU.
rubric.notes: Greedy decoding streams tokens live into the output panel.

rubric.evidence: conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls: searchable source/target <datalist> language pickers (50 languages) that display the resolved code and mark aria-invalid on a bad entry, a Swap button (aria-label), a source textarea, a Beams range slider with a live label, quick-pick chips, and the Translate button. Driving Translate produced the real French output; the pickers resolve names↔codes.
rubric.notes: Pickers use native <datalist> for an accessible searchable combobox; the Swap button has min tap size ≥2.4rem.

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. The output panel only shows text streamed from the pipeline; the see-inside 'Forced BOS' value is the REAL numeric token id read back from the tokenizer (250008 for fr_XX, verified in-browser), not a fabricated number — it shows '–' if the lookup ever fails. Token counts come from the real tokenizer.
rubric.notes: The forced-BOS mechanism is shown honestly with the actual id.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt=pass (no ; icon is an inline SVG data URI), buttons-have-names=pass (Swap button has aria-label). Language pickers have visible
rubric.notes: Applied accessibility guidance: accessible names, aria-invalid on invalid language input, live regions for async state.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. responsive-check desktop (1280×800) + mobile (360×740 DPR3) both clean — no horizontal overflow; the full-lifecycle run reported mobileOverflow=false. Screenshots read: desktop light+dark and a forced light-theme desktop/mobile shot (_runtest/shots/mbart-translation-*) — the at-a-glance table, lang-row grid, tr-io grid and stat-row all wrap cleanly; long tokens use overflow-wrap:anywhere.
rubric.notes: The lang-row 1fr/auto/1fr grid + min-inline-size:0 keeps the pickers from overflowing on 360px.

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs in a Web Worker (MBartEngine → worker.js) and tokens stream via TextStreamer, so the control UI stays responsive during translation. Per-keystroke picker work is O(1) code resolution.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + streamed incremental output.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive translation control panel with language pickers without horizontal overflow on mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use grid with intrinsic tracks and repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) for control/stat rows; set min-inline-size:0 (and overflow-wrap:anywhere) on flex/grid items holding long unbreakable content; use logical properties.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the lang-row is a 1fr/auto/1fr grid with min-inline-size:0 on the picker labels; tr-io and stat-row use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)); the output panel and relay steps use overflow-wrap:anywhere so long tokens/scripts never force horizontal overflow on mobile.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible searchable language combobox and async status announcement
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use native form controls with visible labels; expose validity (aria-invalid); give icon-only buttons an aria-label; announce async state in role=status aria-live.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the source/target pickers are native <input list> comboboxes with <label>s, show their resolved code, and set aria-invalid on a bad language; the Swap button has aria-label; loader + run status use role=status aria-live=polite.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names=pass; live-status-region=pass; aria-invalid toggling in bindPicker

guidanceConsulted.query: stream translation tokens without INP regressions
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + pipeline().generate run in a Web Worker; decoded tokens stream incrementally to the output; picker input handlers do only O(1) code resolution.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js drives pipeline() with a TextStreamer

guidanceConsulted.query: avoid blocking the main thread during translation decoding
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — decoding is off-thread and streamed; there is no long synchronous main-thread loop; render work is bounded by the (short) output length.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js streaming callbacks update the DOM in small increments

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); use-case cards use the shared .model-card grid.

openQuestions: mBART-50 (50 languages) sits alongside NLLB-200 (200) and M2M100 (100) — is the denoising-lineage framing enough to make it feel distinct to a visitor, or should the model page add an explicit 'how mBART differs from M2M/NLLB' comparison box?
Would beam search (num_beams>1, already wired) meaningfully improve quality at q8 without hiding the greedy-streaming mechanism, and should a small beam default be offered?
Should the multi-model page cache-share the Kokoro voice list so switching demos doesn't re-fetch it?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for mbart-translation done (desktop real EN→FR run with forced-BOS 250008 shown + mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read; cached auto-init verified); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.
Add a NEW additive demo directly comparing mBART-50, M2M100 and NLLB-200 on the same sentence + pair, to make the many-to-many lineage differences concrete (new slug, never replacing this page).

summary: Revision-1 build critique for mbart-translation (translation, WASM q8, Xenova/mbart-large-50-many-to-many-mmt), evidenced by a real headless full-lifecycle run: first-visit download (~850 MB) → REAL translation 'Le temps est agréable aujourd'hui.' (en_XX → fr_XX) with the real forced-BOS token id 250008 read back and shown; cached reload auto-inits with no Download button and 0 console errors. The star is the many-to-many forced-BOS mechanism plus mBART's distinct denoising-pretraining lineage vs the built M2M100/Marian/NLLB translators. Conformance 14/14 auto-checkable pass + 9 manual-evidenced; responsive desktop+mobile clean, light+dark legible.
